D1_council_health.note: Unchanged from 0.2.12. Parser fixes (W1, W2) repaired code behavior but did not change member operational status data that drives the health formula. Score honestly reflects substrate state.

gotcha_registry_consulted: G070 — cleared __pycache__ after src/aho/ imports during council health measurement
G079 — baseline_regression_check is backstop: 12 actual failures all within 13 known baseline
G081 — no celebratory framing in any deliverable; substrate findings front-and-center
G082 — canonical paths: event log at ~/.local/share/aho/events/ per canonical resolution
G083 — no new code written; close artifacts only

f1_health_unchanged.detail: Council health 35.3/100 — unchanged from 0.2.12. Success criterion (>=50) not met. This is honest: parser fixes make failures visible rather than silent, but the models themselves remain non-functional. Health score reflects member status, which correctly hasn't changed.

f2_checkpoint_inconsistency.detail: Checkpoint file (.aho-checkpoint.json) had reverted to partially incoherent state between hygiene session and W10 launch. W0 and W1 showed 'in_progress', W3-W6 showed 'in_progress'. Re-corrected to reconciled state at W10 start. Root cause: likely manual edit or another process writing to checkpoint between sessions.

f3_missing_workstream_start_events.detail: No workstream_start events exist in the event log for any 0.2.13 workstream. workstream_complete events exist (from hygiene session) but lifecycle is cosmetically incomplete. Carried forward as protocol gap.

f4_plan_doc_workstream_count_discrepancy.detail: Plan doc footer says '11 (W0, W1, W2, W2.5, W3, W4, W5, W6, W7, W8, W8.5, W9, W10)' which is actually 13 workstreams, not 11. This is a pre-existing error in the plan doc. Per ADR-012, plan doc is immutable — noting it here, not correcting it.
